first_paragraph: The [2012 season](/wiki/2012_NFL_season "2012 NFL season") was the [St. Louis Rams](/wiki/St._Louis_Rams "St. Louis Rams")' 75th in the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL), their 18th in [St. Louis](/wiki/St._Louis,_Missouri "St. Louis, Missouri") and their first under head coach [Jeff Fisher](/wiki/Jeff_Fisher "Jeff Fisher"). Finishing at 7–8–1, they improved on their 2–14 record from [2011](/wiki/2011_St._Louis_Rams_season "2011 St. Louis Rams season"). In Week 10 against the [San Francisco 49ers](/wiki/San_Francisco_49ers "San Francisco 49ers"), the game ended in a 24–24 tie, the first since the [2008 NFL season](/wiki/2008_NFL_season "2008 NFL season"). It was [Sam Bradford](/wiki/Sam_Bradford "Sam Bradford")'s second and final full season as the Rams starting quarterback as two torn ACLs sidelined him for much of [the next season](/wiki/2013_NFL_season "2013 NFL season") and the entire [2014 season](/wiki/2014_NFL_season "2014 NFL season").

The [2012 season](/wiki/2012_NFL_season "2012 NFL season") was the [St. Louis Rams](/wiki/St._Louis_Rams "St. Louis Rams")' 75th in the [National Football League](/wiki/National_Football_League "National Football League") (NFL), their 18th in [St. Louis](/wiki/St._Louis,_Missouri "St. Louis, Missouri") and their first under head coach [Jeff Fisher](/wiki/Jeff_Fisher "Jeff Fisher"). Finishing at 7–8–1, they improved on their 2–14 record from [2011](/wiki/2011_St._Louis_Rams_season "2011 St. Louis Rams season"). In Week 10 against the [San Francisco 49ers](/wiki/San_Francisco_49ers "San Francisco 49ers"), the game ended in a 24–24 tie, the first since the [2008 NFL season](/wiki/2008_NFL_season "2008 NFL season"). It was [Sam Bradford](/wiki/Sam_Bradford "Sam Bradford")'s second and final full season as the Rams starting quarterback as two torn ACLs sidelined him for much of [the next season](/wiki/2013_NFL_season "2013 NFL season") and the entire [2014 season](/wiki/2014_NFL_season "2014 NFL season").

## Personnel changes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2012_St._Louis_Rams_season&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Personnel changes")]

On January 2, 2012, Rams' owner [Stan Kroenke](/wiki/Stan_Kroenke "Stan Kroenke") fired head coach [Steve Spagnuolo](/wiki/Steve_Spagnuolo "Steve Spagnuolo") and general manager [Billy Devaney](/wiki/Billy_Devaney "Billy Devaney") after a 2–14 season, and their 10–38 record after three seasons.[[1]](#cite_note-1)
Former [Tennessee Titans](/wiki/Tennessee_Titans "Tennessee Titans") head coach [Jeff Fisher](/wiki/Jeff_Fisher "Jeff Fisher") was signed to be the new head coach and [Les Snead](/wiki/Les_Snead "Les Snead") was hired to be the new general manager.

The Rams started their season on the road against the Lions. The Rams drew first blood in the first quarter with a 48-yard field goal from Greg Zuerlein to take a 3–0 lead for the only score of that quarter. The Lions then took a 7–3 lead with Joique Bell's 1-yard run for a touchdown in the 2nd quarter. The Rams responded with a 29-yard field goal from Zuerlein as they came up within a point 7–6 before on the Lions' next possession, Matthew Stafford was picked off by Cortland Finnegan and it was returned 31 yards for a touchdown as the Rams retook the lead 13–7. The Lions then responded with Jason Hanson kicking a 41-yard field goal to shorten the Rams' lead to 13–10 at halftime. After the break, the Lions scored first with Jason Hanson kicking a 45-yard field goal to tie the game 13–13 for the only score of the 3rd quarter. However, the Rams moved back into the lead in the 4th quarter with Brandon Gibson's 23-yard catch from Sam Bradford to make it 20–13. The Lions tied the game back up with Kevin Smith running for a touchdown from 5 yards out to tie the game 20–20. The Rams then retook the lead with Greg Zuerlein kicking a 46-yard field goal to make the score 23–20. On their last possession, the Lions moved down the field and Matthew Stafford found Kevin Smith on a 5-yard touchdown pass to make the final score 27–23 as the Rams began their season 0–1.

After defeating the Redskins at home, the Rams traveled on the road against the Bears. The Bears scored first in the first quarter with a 54-yard field goal from Robbie Gould to take a 3–0 lead for the only score of the quarter. In the 2nd quarter, the Bears increased their lead when Michael Bush scored a touchdown on a 5-yard run to make the score 10–0. The Rams responded with Greg Zuerlein's 56-yard field goal to shorten the lead to 10–3 at halftime. In the 3rd quarter, the Rams drew closer as Zuerlein kicked a 46-yard field goal to make the score 10–6 for the only score of the quarter. But in the 4th quarter, the Bears held on for victory as Gould kicked a 22-yard field goal to make it 13–6 and then on the Rams' next possession, Sam Bradford was intercepted by Major Wright who then returned it 45 yards for a touchdown to make it 20–6. Gould kicked a 37-yard field goal to make the final score 23–6 as the Rams dropped to 1–2.

With the road win, the Rams improved their record to 6–6–1. The win was attributed to a late fourth quarter comeback drive by Rams quarterback [Sam Bradford](/wiki/Sam_Bradford "Sam Bradford"). The drive resulted in a touchdown reception by [Brandon Gibson](/wiki/Brandon_Gibson "Brandon Gibson"), bringing the score to 13–12. The Rams then scored a 2-point conversion on a quick pass to [Danny Amendola](/wiki/Danny_Amendola "Danny Amendola"), making the score 15–12 Rams. With under 2 minutes left in the game, the Bills attempted a comeback of their own, but came up short in what resulted in the Rams win.
